Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Did Plato write the Nicomachean Ethics?

No. The Nicomachean Ethics (尼各馬可倫理學) was written by Aristotle, not Plato. Although Aristotle was Plato's most famous student and studied at the Academy for some twenty years, he went on to develop his own distinct philosophical system. The Nicomachean Ethics is one of Aristotle's foundational works, laying out his theory of virtue, eudaimonia (flourishing), and moral character. The title is thought to refer either to his father Nicomachus or his son of the same name. Attributing it to Plato would conflate two of antiquity's greatest but philosophically divergent thinkers.
